violations.text: The menu board is lacking a proper consumer advisory pertaining to the salmon lox (served raw/undercooked). A written consumer advisory (on the menu, table tent, or placard) informing the consumer of the significantly increased risk associated with certain vulnerable consumers eating such food in a raw or undercooked form. Consumer advisory is to be added. New Violation. Correct By: 01/28/2026

At the time of inspection, cream cheeses packaged/repackaged in the food establishment are lacking complete label information. Food packaged in a food establishment must be labeled with the common name of the food, a list of ingredients (sub ingredients and allergens), the quantity of the contents, and the business name/address. New Violation. Correct By: 01/28/2026

The door to the outside in the backroom area was observed to have a gap at the base when closed. Door gap is to be sealed tightly fitting to prevent possible of entry of pests. New Violation. Correct By: 01/28/2026

The hand sink in the backroom area was observed to be pulling away/loosely attached to the wall. Hand sink is to be reattached/installed to ensure continued availability for proper use. New Violation. Correct By: 01/28/2026

The non-food contact surfaces of the back hand sink and the deli slicer were observed to have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. Non-food-contact surfaces of equipment are to be cleaned at a frequency necessary to keep them clean and to preclude the buildup of debris. (PIC cleaned surfaces at time of inspection.) Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 01/14/2026

The dumpster outside is located on gravel/stone. The outdoor storage surface for refuse, recyclables, and returnables must be constructed of nonabsorbent material such as concrete or asphalt and must be smooth, durable, and sloped to drain. New Violation. Correct By: 05/31/2026
Areas of the backroom walls were observed to have surfaces in poor repair (cracked/holes). The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 04/30/2026

The floor in the backroom dry ingredients' storage area and the ceiling to the walk-in cooler were observed with a buildup of dust and debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 01/17/2026

No person in charge certified in food safety was present as needed at the time of inspection. The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. New Violation. Correct By: 09/03/2024

The door to the outside, located in the back storage area, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 09/03/2024
The receptacle in the unisex toilet room is lacking a cover. A toilet room used by females shall be provided with a covered receptacle for sanitary napkins. New Violation. Correct By: 08/27/2024

The dumpster outside is located on gravel/stone. The outdoor storage surface for refuse, recyclables, and returnables must be constructed of nonabsorbent material such as concrete or asphalt and must be smooth, durable, and sloped to drain. New Violation. Correct By: 12/31/2024

The wall between the ice machine and the storage shelving in the back area is in poor repair (cracked/holes). The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 12/31/2024

Chicken Salad and Sliced Onion were observed held at 48 and 51 degrees Fahrenheit respectively in the front service prep unit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. Chicken Salad and Onion were disposed of at the time of inspection. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 06/27/2023

At the time of inspection, Cream cheeses that are taken form original manufacturers packaging and packaged/repackaged in the establishment were observed marked with a use by date longer than 7 days from opening of original packaging. Product packaging use by dates were all appropriately adjusted to 7 days from date of packaging/repackaging at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/27/2023

At the time of inspection, cream cheeses packaged/repackaged in the food establishment are lacking complete label information. Food packaged in a food establishment must be labeled with the common name of the food, a list of ingredients, the quantity of the contents, and the business name/address. Per the PIC, a label making machine has been obtained and complete lists of ingredients are currently being organized. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 07/11/2023

The door to the outside, located in the back area, is not tight-fitting at the base. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. Per the PIC, currently working on getting a new gasket for the door. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 07/11/2023

At the time of inspection, an allergen awareness statement was not observed available for customer viewing. An allergen awareness statement is to be made and viewable to the public. New Violation. Correct By: 06/12/2023

Sliced Cheese in the Prep Unit was observed held at 51 to 54 degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. The vents to the toaster unit located next to the prep unit were observed to be blowing directly towards the foods in the prep unit with the prep unit lid open. (Cheese was voluntarily disposed of at the time of inspection.) Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/12/2023

Food items packaged/repackaged in the food establishment (pastries and cream cheeses) are lacking proper labels. Food packaged in a food establishment must be labeled with the common name of the food, a list of ingredients, the quantity of the contents, and the business name/address. New Violation. Correct By: 06/26/2023

The doors to the outside, located in the Back Prep Area and the Front Entrance, are not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 06/26/2023
The gasket to the walk-in cooler door was observed to be in poor repair (torn/ripped). Equipment is to be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 06/26/2023

The wall and coving in the back prep area behind the ice machine and next to the swinging door are in poor repair. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 07/31/2023
